mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it suggests an accommodative monetary policy in the face of inflationary pressures, which typically boosts equity valuations as real rates decline, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a rebound in housing starts (HOUST) indicates renewed confidence and investment in the real estate sector, which broadly supports economic growth and short-term equity market sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with HOUST growth during identified post-recession recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which underpins consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with the 3-month average growth rate of PAYEMS over a sustained period of 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall economic activity, indicating reduced corporate revenues and profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:industrial activity as economic health proxy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where INDPRO shows a significant consecutive monthly decline.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is low, it indicates an economy with momentum that is not yet constrained by tight monetary policy, leading to an attractive environment for equities and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:growth with low rates is bullish
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 45% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increases borrowing costs and reduces liquidity, which typically puts downward pressure on equity valuations in the short term, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25pp in a single month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a 0.25pp FEDFUNDS hike during a tightening cycle.
